summary: The persisted dogfood benchmark already existed as a task snapshot; the missing piece was making it behave like the rest of the benchmark program, with explicit prompt modes, a stable family alias, and regression checks that exercise oracle-mode verification instead of only the default path.
ergonomics: `benchmarks/run-benchmark.mjs` already had prompt-variant fallback support, so adding mode-specific persistence prompts was low-friction once the task shape was understood.
`benchmarks/test-task-prep.sh` is the right place to lock prompt-selection and mutation-surface expectations, but it is long enough that finding the right extension point takes a few passes.
The existing `benchmarks/test-persistence-benchmarks.sh` made it easy to add a scenario-level oracle-mode check without touching app code.
follow_ups: Add a mirrored Clasp SQLite-backed dogfood benchmark so persisted app changes can participate in the same public comparison rollups as the stateless app tasks.
Consider a dedicated repeated-run alias or scorecard bucket for persisted dogfood benchmarks if more SQLite-backed app tasks are added.
If more single-task benchmark families land, factor the prompt-mode assertions in `benchmarks/test-task-prep.sh` into a helper to avoid copy-paste growth.
warnings: `ts-lead-persistence` previously fell back to `prompt.md` for every mode, so benchmark runs could silently claim oracle/file-hinted coverage without task-specific prompt text unless these variant files exist.

`oracle-check` result selection in `benchmarks/test-task-prep.sh` assumes the latest result for that harness belongs to the just-run task; keep the cleanup in place if more oracle checks are added nearby.
`bash scripts/verify-all.sh` is expensive and emits a lot of dirty-tree warnings from the harness path; that noise can hide benchmark regressions if you are not watching for nonzero exits.
residual_risks: The SQLite-backed benchmark family is still TypeScript-only; there is no mirrored Clasp persistence task for cross-language comparison yet.
This slice adds benchmark-mode and verification coverage around the persisted dogfood task, but it does not introduce a second persisted product-change task beyond `ts-lead-persistence` itself.
